sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the maze structure more clearly. The blue block is at rows 8-12, cols 20-24 (top-right chamber). Looking at the ascii:
- Rows 8-12: The blue block is in a chamber. To the right (cols 24+) there are black walls and a red vertical strip.
- Row 9-11: ...BBRRRBBW - there's a red vertical strip at cols 35-37
- Row 14-18: BBWWWWWBBBBBBBWWWWWWWBRBWWW - white area at cols 20-24 and cols 30-37
- Row 26-37: Large white area at cols 20-37
- Row 38-42: Red horizontal barrier
- Row 42+: White area below the barrier
The blue block is at rows 8-12, cols 20-24. Looking at the maze:
- Below the block (rows 14-18), there's a white area at cols 20-24 (BBWWWWW...)
- But rows 13 and 19 have black walls (BBBBBBB...)
- So the block can move DOWN from rows 8-12 to rows 14-18
Wait, but earlier when I was at rows 14-18, cols 14-18 (left corridor), RIGHT didn't work. But now I'm at rows 8-12, cols 20-24 (right chamber). Let me check if I can move DOWN from here.
Looking at the ascii:
- Row 14: BBWWWWWBBBBBBBWWWWWWWBRBWWW
  - Cols 18-24: BBWWWWW (white at cols 20-24)
  - Cols 24-30: BBBBBBB (black wall)
  - Cols 30-37: WWWWWWW (white)
So there's a white area at cols 20-24 in rows 14-18. The blue block can move DOWN from rows 8-12 to rows 14-18.
But wait, looking at the earlier ascii when the block was at rows 14-18, cols 14-18:
- Row 14: WBbbbbbBWWWWWBBBBB
  - Cols 14-18: bbbbbb (blue block)
  - Cols 19: B (black)
  - Cols 20-24: WWWWB (white)
So the white area at cols 20-24 is to the RIGHT of the left corridor. But RIGHT from the left corridor didn't work. This is confusing.
Actually, I think the issue is that the blue block moves in 6-row or 6-col jumps, and it can only move to adjacent chambers that are connected by passages. The left corridor (cols 14-18) and the right chamber (cols 20-24) are separate chambers connected only at the top (rows 8-12).
So from the top-right chamber (rows 8-12, cols 20-24), I can:
1. Move LEFT back to top-left (rows 8-12, cols 14-18)
2. Move DOWN to the chamber below (rows 14-18, cols 20-24)
Let me try DOWN to explore the right side of the maze.

</parameter>
